Nichtsdestotrotz sind die Arbeiten im Weinberg bis zur Weinlese sehr vielfältig: Bodenbearbeitung, Rebschnitt, Binden, Düngen, Laubarbeiten, Reberziehung Die Arbeiter von Wien Das Lied Die Arbeiter von Wien entstand vermutlich 1927, als während der so genannten Julirevolte in Wien 89 Demonstranten durch Schüsse in die Menge getötet wurden. Der Text des Arbeiterliedes stammt von Fritz Brügel (1897–1955), einem Wiener Lyriker und Essayisten. "Workers of Vienna" is an Austrian workers' song written by Fritz Brügel. It uses the tune of "White Army, Black Baron" (A Bolsheviks' Russian marching song), composed by Samuel Pokrass. It became popular during the civil war, and its popularity has since grown, attracting many anti-fascist movements and people around the world. Der Text stammt von Fritz Brügel (1897–1955), einem Wiener Lyriker und Essayisten. Work in the Vineyard Many stages of work are involved before a finished bottle of wine reaches a consumer. Throughout the entire year a wine-grower has various tasks to tend to, such as training and pruning the vines, soil care, foliage treatment and thinning, as well as harvesting the grapes as selectively as possible.
